Why mild annoyance at Trump won't help Keir Starmer
description: Trump has treated the UK the way he has treated Europe, NATO and anyone else he thinks is not carrying its water. Starmer, in contrast, has tried to draw a line. He has said Britain will not repeat the “mistakes of Iraq” and will act only on a “lawful basis”. Even that, however, pales in comparison to the bluntness coming out of Europe. France’s Emmanuel Macron has openly mocked Trump’s inconsistency, saying “you have to be serious” and warning that a leader “can’t contradict himself every day.” Against that, Starmer’s irritation feels less like defiance and more like discomfort.

Google AI CEO Demis Hassabis says ChatGPT did something in 2022 that can't be undone
description: Demis Hassabis, CEO of Google DeepMind, says ChatGPT's viral 2022 launch triggered a "ferocious commercial pressure race" that nobody—including OpenAI—was prepared for. Speaking to Cleo Abram, he said every major lab had equivalent systems at the time; OpenAI just shipped first. Hassabis had wanted AI kept in the lab longer, focused on scientific breakthroughs. Now he's racing alongside everyone else—and watching the guardrails get harder to build.

Kissa cough syrup ka: When medicine is cast as contraband
description: India's push for 'Jan Vishwas' faces a stark contradiction as authorities increasingly use stringent NDPS and BNS laws against licensed pharmaceutical stockists. Despite clear regulations under the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, businesses dealing in common cough syrups are being prosecuted, blurring lines between regulation and criminality. This trend risks eroding trust and undermining the rule of law.

‘This isn’t education’: Student made to fan headmistress; video goes viral
description: A viral video from Rewa district shows a government middle school headmistress, Varsha Manjhi, being fanned by a student during class. The incident has sparked outrage, with locals condemning it as exploitation. Authorities are investigating and promise strict action.

title: Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry make noble move, continue giving back
description: Stephen Curry, the NBA legend, sparked shockwaves when he ended his decade long partnership with Under Armour in 2025, after over 10 years of being with the brand exclusively. While Stephen Curry remained vague about the reason behind it, it seems there was a pretty noble thought that led to this decision and his wife, Ayesha Curry, has been a supporter through all of it.

title: Dianna Russini’s ‘Miami’ remark sparks outrage; denies Vrabel affair
description: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and The New York Times’ popular NFL reporter and insider, Dianna Russini, have sparked outrage among millions after pictures of them sitting together at an upscale hotel were leaked to the media. Both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have strongly denied any rumors of an affair and continue to maintain they were in a professional setting surrounded by other people. Amid all the mess, Dianna Russini’s old remarks have raised eyebrows.

title: Two years after Google CEO Sundar Pichai’s ‘Code Red’, Satya Nadella orders AI overhaul
description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has initiated a 'Copilot code red' to enhance AI performance and user experience, aiming to boost investor confidence amidst intense competition.  The company is allocating significant cloud capacity to Copilot development, with analysts suggesting Azure can still exceed expectations due to growing demand and pricing.

title: Who is Jennifer Vrabel? Mike Vrabel’s wife amid affair rumours
description: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, has sparked outrage as many are convinced that he has allegedly cheated on his wife, Jennifer Vrabel, with The New York Times’ Dianna Russini. While both Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have strongly denied affair rumors, the head coach’s wife has stayed out of the spotlight even as they have been married for 27 years.
